# 🎛️ WaveX Unified Input Demo (C++ + LVGL)

## 1. Directory Layout

```
/ui
 ├── input_event.h
 ├── input_manager.h / .cpp
 ├── ui_context.h
 ├── ui_patch_list.cpp
 ├── ui_param_edit.cpp
 ├── ui_main.cpp

---

## 2. `input_event.h`

```cpp
#pragma once
#include <cstdint>

enum class InputType {
    TouchDown,
    TouchUp,
    TouchMove,
    ButtonPress,
    ButtonRelease,
    EncoderLeft,
    EncoderRight,
    EncoderClick
};

struct InputEvent {
    InputType type;
    uint8_t source_id = 0;
    int16_t delta = 0;
    uint16_t x = 0, y = 0;
    uint32_t timestamp_ms = 0;
};
```

---

## 3. `ui_context.h`

```cpp
#pragma once
#include "input_event.h"
#include <functional>
#include <string>

using InputHandler = std::function<void(const InputEvent&)>;

class UIContext {
public:
    UIContext(std::string name, InputHandler handler)
        : name_(std::move(name)), handler_(std::move(handler)) {}

    void handleEvent(const InputEvent& evt) const {
        if (handler_) handler_(evt);
    }

private:
    std::string name_;
    InputHandler handler_;
};
```

---

## 4. `input_manager.h` + `.cpp`

```cpp
// input_manager.h
#pragma once
#include "ui_context.h"
#include <queue>
#include <mutex>
#include <memory>

class InputManager {
public:
    static InputManager& instance();

    void setActiveContext(std::shared_ptr<UIContext> ctx);
    void postEvent(const InputEvent& evt);
    void processEvents();

private:
    InputManager() = default;
    std::queue<InputEvent> eventQueue_;
    std::shared_ptr<UIContext> currentContext_;
    std::mutex mutex_;
};
```

```cpp
// input_manager.cpp
#include "input_manager.h"

InputManager& InputManager::instance() {
    static InputManager inst;
    return inst;
}

void InputManager::setActiveContext(std::shared_ptr<UIContext> ctx) {
    std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
    currentContext_ = std::move(ctx);
}

void InputManager::postEvent(const InputEvent& evt) {
    std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
    eventQueue_.push(evt);
}

void InputManager::processEvents() {
    std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
    while (!eventQueue_.empty()) {
        auto evt = eventQueue_.front();
        eventQueue_.pop();
        if (currentContext_) currentContext_->handleEvent(evt);
    }
}
```

---

## 5. `ui_patch_list.cpp`

```cpp
#include "ui_context.h"
#include "input_manager.h"
#include "lvgl.h"
#include <memory>

extern std::shared_ptr<UIContext> createParamEditContext();

// forward-declared UI elements
static lv_obj_t* list = nullptr;

static void patchListHandler(const InputEvent& evt) {
    switch (evt.type) {
        case InputType::EncoderLeft:
            lv_group_focus_prev(lv_group_get_default());
            break;
        case InputType::EncoderRight:
            lv_group_focus_next(lv_group_get_default());
            break;
        case InputType::ButtonPress:
        case InputType::EncoderClick:
            // simulate "select patch"
            InputManager::instance().setActiveContext(createParamEditContext());
            break;
        default:
            break;
    }
}

std::shared_ptr<UIContext> createPatchListContext() {
    return std::make_shared<UIContext>("PatchList", patchListHandler);
}

lv_obj_t* createPatchListPage(lv_obj_t* parent) {
    list = lv_list_create(parent);
    lv_obj_set_size(list, 480, 320);
    for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i) {
        lv_list_add_text(list, ("Patch " + std::to_string(i)).c_str());
    }
    lv_group_add_obj(lv_group_get_default(), list);
    return list;
}
```

---

## 6. `ui_param_edit.cpp`

```cpp
#include "ui_context.h"
#include "input_manager.h"
#include "lvgl.h"
#include <memory>

extern std::shared_ptr<UIContext> createPatchListContext();

static lv_obj_t* labelValue = nullptr;
static int paramValue = 50;

static void updateLabel() {
    char buf[32];
    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "Parameter = %d", paramValue);
    lv_label_set_text(labelValue, buf);
}

static void paramEditHandler(const InputEvent& evt) {
    switch (evt.type) {
        case InputType::EncoderLeft:
            paramValue = std::max(0, paramValue - 1);
            updateLabel();
            break;
        case InputType::EncoderRight:
            paramValue = std::min(127, paramValue + 1);
            updateLabel();
            break;
        case InputType::ButtonPress:
        case InputType::EncoderClick:
            InputManager::instance().setActiveContext(createPatchListContext());
            break;
        default:
            break;
    }
}

std::shared_ptr<UIContext> createParamEditContext() {
    return std::make_shared<UIContext>("ParamEdit", paramEditHandler);
}

lv_obj_t* createParamEditPage(lv_obj_t* parent) {
    labelValue = lv_label_create(parent);
    lv_obj_center(labelValue);
    updateLabel();
    return labelValue;
}
```

---

## 7. `ui_main.cpp`

This sets up the LVGL screens and initializes the first context.

```cpp
#include "input_manager.h"
#include "ui_context.h"
#include "lvgl.h"
#include <memory>

extern lv_obj_t* createPatchListPage(lv_obj_t*);
extern lv_obj_t* createParamEditPage(lv_obj_t*);
extern std::shared_ptr<UIContext> createPatchListContext();

static lv_obj_t* main_screen = nullptr;

void ui_init() {
    main_screen = lv_obj_create(nullptr);
    createPatchListPage(main_screen);
    lv_scr_load(main_screen);

    InputManager::instance().setActiveContext(createPatchListContext());
}
```

---

## 8. `ui_task.cpp`

Run the LVGL task loop and event processing.

```cpp
#include "input_manager.h"
#include "lvgl.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

extern void ui_init();

extern "C" void ui_task(void*) {
    ui_init();

    while (true) {
        lv_timer_handler();
        InputManager::instance().processEvents();
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10));
    }
}
```

---

## 9. Example ISR or Poll Hooks

These would be your hardware drivers or FT6336/PCNT callbacks:

```cpp
void encoderCallback(int dir) {
    InputEvent evt;
    evt.type = (dir > 0) ? InputType::EncoderRight : InputType::EncoderLeft;
    evt.timestamp_ms = esp_timer_get_time() / 1000;
    InputManager::instance().postEvent(evt);
}

void buttonCallback(bool pressed) {
    InputEvent evt;
    evt.type = pressed ? InputType::ButtonPress : InputType::ButtonRelease;
    evt.timestamp_ms = esp_timer_get_time() / 1000;
    InputManager::instance().postEvent(evt);
}
```

# ✅ Behavior Summary

| Input Device | Patch List Page            | Param Edit Page            |
| ------------ | -------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| Encoder      | Scrolls through list items | Adjusts parameter value    |
| Button       | Selects patch              | Returns to patch list      |
| Touch        | Can still interact w/ LVGL | Optional parameter control |

<details>
        <summary>Apply patch for /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/input_dispatcher.cpp</summary>
      

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/input_dispatcher.cpp
+#include "ui/input_dispatcher.h"
+#include "esp_timer.h"
+
+namespace wavex_ui {
+
+static constexpr size_t kQueueLength = 64;
+
+InputDispatcher& InputDispatcher::instance() {
+    static InputDispatcher inst;
+    return inst;
+}
+
+InputDispatcher::InputDispatcher() {
+    queue_ = xQueueCreate(kQueueLength, sizeof(InputEvent));
+}
+
+bool InputDispatcher::postFromISR(const InputEvent& evt, BaseType_t* hpTaskWoken) {
+    if (!queue_) return false;
+    return xQueueSendFromISR(queue_, &evt, hpTaskWoken) == pdTRUE;
+}
+
+bool InputDispatcher::post(const InputEvent& evt, TickType_t ticksToWait) {
+    if (!queue_) return false;
+    return xQueueSend(queue_, &evt, ticksToWait) == pdTRUE;
+}
+
+void InputDispatcher::processAll() {
+    if (!queue_) return;
+    InputEvent evt;
+    while (xQueueReceive(queue_, &evt, 0) == pdTRUE) {
+        if (current_) {
+            current_->handleEvent(evt);
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+void InputDispatcher::setActiveContext(std::shared_ptr<UIContext> ctx) {
+    current_ = std::move(ctx);
+}
+
+} // namespace wavex_ui
+
*** End Patch
```

<details>
        <summary>Apply patch for /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/ui_patch_list.cpp</summary>
      

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/ui_patch_list.cpp
+#include <memory>
+#include <string>
+#include "lvgl.h"
+#include "ui/input_dispatcher.h"
+#include "ui/ui_demo.h"
+
+namespace wavex_ui {
+
+static lv_obj_t* s_list = nullptr;
+
+static void patchListHandler(const InputEvent& evt) {
+    switch (evt.type) {
+        case InputType::EncoderLeft:
+            lv_group_focus_prev(lv_group_get_default());
+            break;
+        case InputType::EncoderRight:
+            lv_group_focus_next(lv_group_get_default());
+            break;
+        case InputType::ButtonPress:
+        case InputType::EncoderClick:
+            InputDispatcher::instance().setActiveContext(createParamEditContext());
+            break;
+        default:
+            break;
+    }
+}
+
+std::shared_ptr<UIContext> createPatchListContext() {
+    return std::make_shared<UIContext>("PatchList", patchListHandler);
+}
+
+lv_obj_t* createPatchListPage(lv_obj_t* parent) {
+    s_list = lv_list_create(parent);
+    lv_obj_set_size(s_list, lv_pct(100), lv_pct(100));
+    for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i) {
+        std::string label = std::string("Patch ") + std::to_string(i);
+        lv_list_add_text(s_list, label.c_str());
+    }
+    if (lv_group_get_default()) {
+        lv_group_add_obj(lv_group_get_default(), s_list);
+    }
+    return s_list;
+}
+
+} // namespace wavex_ui
+
*** End Patch
```

Tool use: **apply_patch**

<details>
        <summary>Apply patch for /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/ui_param_edit.cpp</summary>
      

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/ui_param_edit.cpp
+#include <memory>
+#include <algorithm>
+#include "lvgl.h"
+#include "ui/input_dispatcher.h"
+#include "ui/ui_demo.h"
+
+namespace wavex_ui {
+
+static lv_obj_t* s_label_value = nullptr;
+static int s_param_value = 50;
+
+static void updateLabel() {
+    if (!s_label_value) return;
+    char buf[32];
+    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "Parameter = %d", s_param_value);
+    lv_label_set_text(s_label_value, buf);
+}
+
+static void paramEditHandler(const InputEvent& evt) {
+    switch (evt.type) {
+        case InputType::EncoderLeft:
+            s_param_value = std::max(0, s_param_value - 1);
+            updateLabel();
+            break;
+        case InputType::EncoderRight:
+            s_param_value = std::min(127, s_param_value + 1);
+            updateLabel();
+            break;
+        case InputType::ButtonPress:
+        case InputType::EncoderClick:
+            InputDispatcher::instance().setActiveContext(createPatchListContext());
+            break;
+        default:
+            break;
+    }
+}
+
+std::shared_ptr<UIContext> createParamEditContext() {
+    return std::make_shared<UIContext>("ParamEdit", paramEditHandler);
+}
+
+lv_obj_t* createParamEditPage(lv_obj_t* parent) {
+    s_label_value = lv_label_create(parent);
+    lv_obj_center(s_label_value);
+    updateLabel();
+    return s_label_value;
+}
+
+} // namespace wavex_ui
+
*** End Patch
```

                 uint32_t current_idx = wavex_file_browser_get_selected_index(s_sample_load_save_page->file_browser);
                 uint32_t max_idx = wavex_file_browser_get_entry_count(s_sample_load_save_page->file_browser);
                 int32_t target = (int32_t)current_idx + enc_delta;
                 if (target < 0) target = 0;
                 if (max_idx > 0 && target > (int32_t)max_idx - 1) target = (int32_t)max_idx - 1;

Tool use: **apply_patch**

<details>
        <summary>Apply patch for /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/tca8418_keypad.cpp</summary>
      

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /workspaces/WaveX/firmware/esp32/components/ui/src/tca8418_keypad.cpp
+#include "ui/tca8418_keypad.h"
+#include "ui/input_dispatcher.h"
+#include "ui/input_event.h"
+#include "esp_log.h"
+#include "esp_check.h"
+#include "driver/gpio.h"
+#include "esp_timer.h"
+#include "bsp/esp32_p4_nano.h"
+#include "esp_tca8418.h"
+
+namespace wavex_ui {
+
+static const char* TAG = "TCA8418";
+static TaskHandle_t s_task = nullptr;
+static gpio_num_t s_int_gpio = GPIO_NUM_NC;
+static tca8418_handle_t s_dev = nullptr;
+
+// Simple example mapping: map TCA keycode -> logical button id
+static uint8_t map_keycode_to_button(uint8_t keycode) {
+    // Keycode per TCA8418: 1..80 => R/C encoded; adjust as needed
+    // Example: return 1 for Select, 2 for Back, 3 for EncoderClick
+    switch (keycode) {
+        case 1: return 1; // Select
+        case 2: return 2; // Back
+        case 3: return 3; // EncoderClick
+        default: return 0; // Unknown
+    }
+}
+
+static void post_button(bool pressed, uint8_t button_id) {
+    if (button_id == 0) return;
+    InputEvent evt{};
+    evt.type = pressed ? InputType::ButtonPress : InputType::ButtonRelease;
+    evt.source_id = button_id;
+    evt.timestamp_ms = (uint32_t)(esp_timer_get_time() / 1000);
+    InputDispatcher::instance().post(evt);
+}
+
+static void keypad_task(void* arg) {
+    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keypad task started");
+    while (true) {
+        // If INT available, wait for it, otherwise poll periodically
+        if (s_int_gpio != GPIO_NUM_NC) {
+            // Simple polling of GPIO level; could use ISR+queue for lower latency
+            if (gpio_get_level(s_int_gpio) == 0) {
+                // INT asserted (active low)
+            } else {
+                vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10));
+                continue;
+            }
+        } else {
+            vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(20));
+        }
+
+        // Drain event FIFO
+        tca8418_event_t ev;
+        while (tca8418_get_event(s_dev, &ev) == ESP_OK) {
+            bool pressed = (ev.type == TCA8418_EVENT_PRESS);
+            uint8_t button = map_keycode_to_button(ev.key);
+            post_button(pressed, button);
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+esp_err_t tca8418_keypad_start(int int_gpio, uint8_t i2c_addr) {
+    if (s_task) return ESP_OK;
+
+    // Use BSP I2C bus
+    i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c = bsp_i2c_get_handle();
+    if (i2c == nullptr) {
+        ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(bsp_i2c_init(), TAG, "Failed to init BSP I2C");
+        i2c = bsp_i2c_get_handle();
+    }
+
+    // Create device
+    tca8418_config_t cfg = {
+        .i2c_bus = i2c,
+        .i2c_addr = i2c_addr,
+        .rows = 8,
+        .cols = 10,
+        .debounce_ms = 5,
+        .autorepeat = false,
+    };
+    ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(tca8418_new(&cfg, &s_dev), TAG, "tca8418_new failed");
+
+    // Configure INT GPIO if provided
+    if (int_gpio >= 0) {
+        s_int_gpio = (gpio_num_t)int_gpio;
+        gpio_config_t io = {};
+        io.pin_bit_mask = 1ULL << s_int_gpio;
+        io.mode = GPIO_MODE_INPUT;
+        io.pull_up_en = GPIO_PULLUP_ENABLE;
+        io.pull_down_en = GPIO_PULLDOWN_DISABLE;
+        io.intr_type = GPIO_INTR_DISABLE;
+        ESP_RETURN_ON_ERROR(gpio_config(&io), TAG, "gpio_config failed");
+    }
+
+    // Start task
+    BaseType_t ok = xTaskCreatePinnedToCore(keypad_task, "tca8418_task", 4096, nullptr, 5, &s_task, 1);
+    if (ok != pdPASS) {
+        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create keypad task");
+        tca8418_del(s_dev);
+        s_dev = nullptr;
+        return ESP_FAIL;
+    }
+    return ESP_OK;
+}
+
+esp_err_t tca8418_keypad_stop() {
+    if (s_task) {
+        vTaskDelete(s_task);
+        s_task = nullptr;
+    }
+    if (s_dev) {
+        tca8418_del(s_dev);
+        s_dev = nullptr;
+    }
+    s_int_gpio = GPIO_NUM_NC;
+    return ESP_OK;
+}
+
+} // namespace wavex_ui
+
*** End Patch
```

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,151 @@
- 
+ <!-- cf54cfa4-f2da-4c82-b541-818cf234cc00 46fbd9da-3d84-460d-99dc-37742f805baa -->
+ # ESP32 Unified Input Subsystem Plan
+ 
+ ## Scope and Assumptions
+ 
+ - Assume LVGL 9.x; if 8.x, adjust `lv_group` and timer APIs accordingly.
+ - Use existing `pcnt_task` as encoder tick source; map ±ticks → left/right events.
+ - TCA8418 keypad IC provides button events via I2C (no GPIO ISR buttons).
+ - Optional touch driver remains independent; can post `Touch*` events later.
+ - Threading: ISRs and driver tasks post to a FreeRTOS queue; a UI task consumes and dispatches into LVGL contexts.
+ 
+ ## Architecture
+ 
+ - Event-centric pipeline:
+   - ISR/drivers → `InputEventQueue` (FreeRTOS `QueueHandle_t`, size ~64)
+   - UI task → `InputDispatcher` (current context) → context handler (patch list or param edit)
+ - Context model:
+   - `UIContext` holds a name and a handler `std::function<void(const InputEvent&)>`
+   - `InputDispatcher` tracks active context (atomic/shared_ptr protected by consumer task only)
+ - LVGL integration:
+   - Dedicated `ui_task` calls `lv_timer_handler()` and drains queue periodically
+   - Encoder movement maps to `lv_group_focus_next/prev` on PatchList; to param increment/decrement on ParamEdit
+ - ISR safety:
+   - Posting API: `postFromISR(InputEvent&)` uses `xQueueSendFromISR`
+   - Driver/task posting: `post(InputEvent&)` uses `xQueueSend`
+ 
+ ## Files and Layout (ESP-IDF component)
+ 
+ - Component `firmware/esp32/components/ui/`
+   - `include/ui/input_event.h`: Input types and event struct
+   - `include/ui/ui_context.h`: Context abstraction
+   - `include/ui/input_dispatcher.h`: Singleton queue + context dispatch
+   - `include/ui/ui_api.h`: Minimal UI API (`ui_init_demo`, context setter)
+   - `include/ui/ui_demo.h`: Demo contexts/pages declarations
+   - `include/ui/tca8418_keypad.h`: Keypad start/stop API
+   - `src/input_dispatcher.cpp`: Queue init, post APIs, process loop
+   - `src/ui_main.cpp`: Demo init and context setter
+   - `src/ui_patch_list.cpp`: Patch List context/page
+   - `src/ui_param_edit.cpp`: Parameter Edit context/page
+   - `src/tca8418_keypad.cpp`: TCA8418 keypad task; posts ButtonPress/Release
+ 
+ ## Data Types (essential snippets)
+ 
+ ```cpp
+ #pragma once
+ #include <cstdint>
+ 
+ enum class InputType : uint8_t {
+   TouchDown, TouchUp, TouchMove,
+   ButtonPress, ButtonRelease,
+   EncoderLeft, EncoderRight, EncoderClick
+ };
+ 
+ struct InputEvent {
+   InputType type;
+   uint8_t source_id = 0;   // e.g., encoder id or button id
+   int16_t delta = 0;       // encoder steps or touch deltas
+   uint16_t x = 0, y = 0;   // touch coords
+   uint32_t timestamp_ms = 0;
+ };
+ ```
+ 
+ ## Input Dispatcher (queue + posting API)
+ 
+ ```cpp
+ #pragma once
+ #include <memory>
+ #include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
+ #include "freertos/queue.h"
+ #include "ui/input_event.h"
+ #include "ui/ui_context.h"
+ 
+ class InputDispatcher {
+ public:
+   static InputDispatcher& instance();
+   bool postFromISR(const InputEvent& evt, BaseType_t* hpTaskWoken);
+   bool post(const InputEvent& evt, TickType_t ticksToWait = 0);
+   void processAll();
+   void setActiveContext(std::shared_ptr<UIContext> ctx);
+ private:
+   InputDispatcher();
+   QueueHandle_t queue_;
+   std::shared_ptr<UIContext> current_;
+ };
+ ```
+ 
+ ## UI Contexts and Screens
+ 
+ - Patch List
+   - LVGL list on main screen, added to default group
+   - Handler: left/right → `lv_group_focus_prev/next`; press/click → switch to ParamEdit
+ - Param Edit
+   - Centered label showing `Parameter = X`
+   - Handler: left/right → decrement/increment with clamping; click/press → back to PatchList
+ - UI Init
+   - Create main screen/page, load, set active context to PatchList
+ 
+ ## UI Task
+ 
+ - Drain encoder deltas → post `EncoderLeft/EncoderRight` events
+ - `InputDispatcher::processAll()` each loop
+ - Delay tuned for LVGL + UI responsiveness
+ 
+ ## Integration Points
+ 
+ - Encoder (`pcnt_task.cpp`):
+   - On delta, construct `InputEvent` with `EncoderRight/Left`, set `delta` and `timestamp_ms`, post to dispatcher
+ - Keypad (TCA8418 over I2C):
+   - Dependency: `atanisoft/esp_tca8418` (~1.0.0) in `idf_component.yml`
+   - Use BSP I2C bus shared with touch (SDA=20, SCL=21)
+   - INT GPIO: 31 (active-low). No reset line used
+   - Drain TCA8418 event FIFO on INT/poll; map keycode → logical button IDs (Select=1, Back=2, EncoderClick=3 by default)
+   - Post `ButtonPress`/`ButtonRelease` with `source_id`
+ - Touch (optional): existing touch task posts `TouchDown/Move/Up`
+ 
+ ## CMake/Component Wiring
+ 
+ - `ui` registers new sources; `REQUIRES esp_tca8418` added
+ - `main` `idf_component.yml` adds `atanisoft/esp_tca8418` dependency
+ - Ensure C++17 and consistent compile options
+ 
+ ## Logging and Metrics
+ 
+ - Low-noise logs for context switches and queue overruns
+ - Counter for dropped input events
+ 
+ ## Testing Strategy
+ 
+ - Inject synthetic events to verify dispatcher/context handling
+ - Manual: encoder rotation, keypad Select/Back/Click → verify page navigation and edits
+ - Stress: rapid encoder ticks and key bursts; verify no UI stutter
+ 
+ ## Future Extensions
+ 
+ - Focus persistence per context
+ - Fine-adjust (press-and-rotate)
+ - Long-press detection in dispatcher
+ - LVGL indev driver bridge for native encoder nav
+ 
+ ## To-dos
+ 
+ - [x] Create `ui` component with headers and sources, plus CMake wiring
+ - [x] Implement `input_event.h` with InputType and InputEvent struct
+ - [x] Implement FreeRTOS-queued `InputDispatcher` with ISR-safe posting
+ - [x] Implement PatchList and ParamEdit contexts and LVGL pages
+ - [x] Add `ui_task` that runs LVGL and processes events
+ - [x] Wire `pcnt_task` ticks to encoder events into dispatcher
+ - [x] Add TCA8418 keypad task to post press/release events
+ - [x] Register component and link with main and LVGL
+ - [ ] Add guarded logs and queue overflow counters
+ - [ ] Run on hardware; validate scrolling/select/edit/back flows
```

</details>
